title: test(clickhouse): stop merges before TRUNCATE, use tmpfs and Docker healthcheck
summary: CI optimization PR from the StarSling bot that speeds up ClickHouse tests by ~82% (134s → 24s avg). Three changes: (1) Issues SYSTEM STOP MERGES before TRUNCATE TABLE in clearTable() to avoid lock contention from background ReplacingMergeTree merges, then restarts merges after. (2) Switches Docker data volume to tmpfs for in-memory I/O. (3) Pins the Docker image to 26.2.3.2, adds a native healthcheck, and simplifies the pretest script from a shell polling loop to `docker compose up -d --wait`. Estimated savings: ~6,490 CI min/month.
maintainerNotes: Well-documented PR with A/B benchmark data (10 runs). CI failures (Observability Package Tests, Full Test Suite) are pre-existing on main and unrelated to this change. No changeset included, but this is test/CI infrastructure only so one may not be needed. The code change in clearTable() is production code (not just test infra) — verify that SYSTEM STOP/START MERGES is safe for production use and won't cause issues if clearTable() throws between stop and start (merges would remain paused). The author is a bot (StarSling CI optimization agent).
